mentions.note: Transport and delivery play; growth and disruption story; good diversifier.
Transport diversification; growth play with improving unit economics.
Caller John (entry at high $80s): Stay in; company taking over the world, short-term volatility temporary, stock too cheap.

Down 25% from September highs; value stock at 23x earnings, cheapest since 2023 profitability; Nvidia backing robo-taxi partnerships provides visibility; fundamental ride-share business strong; partner with autonomous operators rather than compete.
Long-term great value; quasi-monopoly globally; in everyone's app; brand strength worldwide justifies ownership.
Cramer is 'an Uber guy'; loves Trist; thinks very very cheap right here; implied contrast to Lyft which caller sold at top.
Down 29% from Sept 2025 high; 40% EPS growth, robo-taxi fears overblown via Nuro partnership. 200M monthly users create network moat. Cheap for first time in years at 21.3x earnings.
Definitely a buy; before really good quarter at 25x earnings with great growth; great growth not just in data center but away from data center—exactly what market needs.
Stock settled down to $72-77 range after pullback from recent highs; recommend accumulating for 1-2 year hold despite near-term ranging.
